---
title: "The Fairness Contract"
type: primer
provenance: "synthesis"
---

*Silhouette: The promise that a solver who pays attention can always get there from here.*

<!-- Primer: orientation to the Puzzles, Clues & Information family. Light authorial / Mentor voice. -->

## The Terrain

Every puzzle is a transaction between two people who never meet: the designer who hides something, and the solver who comes to find it. What holds that transaction together is a promise. The designer promises that the answer is reachable, that every step is fair, that no solution depends on a rule the solver was never told. The solver, trusting that promise, agrees to think rather than guess. Break the promise once and the trust is gone, and a solver who no longer trusts the puzzle stops reasoning and starts flailing. That promise is the fairness contract, and it is the ground every other puzzle concern stands on.

This family is the craft of investigation and discovery: clues and how they are made sound, the order they arrive in, the kinds of puzzle a designer can reach for, the moment of insight they build toward, and the graceful exit for the solver they failed to reach. The mechanisms differ wildly, a cipher and a scavenger trail and a meta-puzzle have almost nothing mechanical in common, but they all answer to the same contract. Fairness is the floor; everything else is built on it.

The contract is not the same as ease. A fair puzzle can be brutally hard. Fairness is a promise about *reachability*, not about effort: it says the path exists and the solver can find it with attention, not that the path is short. The whole art of this family is asking a great deal of the solver while keeping that promise intact.

::: {.callout-tip}
### Mentor Wisdom
When a puzzle is not landing, the instinct is to add another clue. Resist it. Ask first whether the clue you have is doing its work and whether the solver can even tell it is a clue. A cleaner signal almost always beats a louder one. And when your own notes start saying "but they should have realised," stop: that sentence is usually the designer's voice covering an unfair gap. The solver does not owe you the leap.
:::

## Key Mechanisms

The contract is set by [Cluing & Fairness](../articles/puzzles-clues-information/cluing-and-fairness.qmd): every clue does real work, the answer is reachable, and misdirection always has an exit. Once the clues are sound, the order they arrive in is its own craft, [Clue Sequencing & Chains](../articles/puzzles-clues-information/clue-sequencing-and-chains.qmd), and the scenario-wide map of which clues feed which conclusions is [Information Architecture](../articles/puzzles-clues-information/information-architecture.qmd).

What a designer is actually choosing among is mapped by [A Taxonomy of Puzzles](../articles/puzzles-clues-information/a-taxonomy-of-puzzles.qmd), which sorts the space by what the mind is doing and what job each puzzle does in the hour around it. Two faculties earn their own treatment: the noticing puzzles of [Search, Observation & Lateral Thinking](../articles/puzzles-clues-information/search-observation-and-lateral-thinking.qmd) and the encoded messages of [Ciphers & Codes](../articles/puzzles-clues-information/ciphers-and-codes.qmd). The abstraction type that ends most puzzle sets, the answers-as-data step, is [Extraction, Indexing & Meta-Puzzles](../articles/puzzles-clues-information/extraction-indexing-and-meta-puzzles.qmd), and the puzzle that judges its own solution is [Self-Resolving Puzzles](../articles/puzzles-clues-information/self-resolving-puzzles.qmd).

The contract is also taught and repaired. A good opening teaches the solver how to read the puzzle before it tests them, which is [Teaching Through Play](../articles/puzzles-clues-information/teaching-through-play.qmd), and the reward all of this builds toward is [The Aha Moment](../articles/puzzles-clues-information/the-aha-moment.qmd). When a fair puzzle still leaves a particular solver stuck, the recourse is [Hint Systems & Recovery](../articles/puzzles-clues-information/hint-systems-and-recovery.qmd).

At the scale of whole events the contract shapes formats: the feeders, rounds, and metas of [Puzzle Hunt Craft](../articles/puzzles-clues-information/puzzle-hunt-craft.qmd), the distributed trail of [Treasure & Scavenger Hunts](../articles/puzzles-clues-information/treasure-and-scavenger-hunts.qmd), and the lineage they share in [The Puzzle Hunt & ARG Tradition](../articles/puzzles-clues-information/the-puzzle-hunt-and-arg-tradition.qmd).

## Related Exemplars

The MIT Mystery Hunt is the deepest living laboratory of fair cluing at scale; *Cain's Jawbone* is the fairness contract turned inside out, a solver reconstructing the order the text demands; and the *Unlock!* series shows the contract enforced by a companion app that judges every answer. (Exemplar profiles are written in a later phase; these are pointers for now.)
